supply_chain.dependencies: The company relies on subcontractors and suppliers for raw materials, chemicals, parts, components, subsystems, hardware elements, sub-assemblies, software, intellectual property, and services. Disruptions or performance problems with suppliers can adversely affect ability to meet commitments to customers.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain challenges due to macroeconomic environment including inflation, labor shortages, supply chain disruptions, extended material lead times, microelectronics shortages, sole source suppliers, counterfeit parts risk, supplier financial viability, and inability to procure from certain sources due to regulatory restrictions

regulatory_risks: Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) compliance requirements
Suspension or debarment from government contracts
Defense Contract Audit Agency (DCAA) and Defense Contract Management Agency (DCMA) audits and reviews
Environmental laws and regulations including climate change regulations
California and European Union climate risk reporting requirements
Federal Clean Air Act and Clean Water Act compliance
Cybersecurity and information security regulatory requirements
Tax regulations including IRC Section 174 and Section 451(b)
Pillar Two global minimum tax regulations

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed in countries where company does business or seeks to do business
Impact of geopolitical conflicts on supply chain and operations
Foreign government subsidies to competitors creating unequal competition
